projectDescription: A high-performance Python library for manipulating 2-dimensional tabular data structures (DataFrames), optimized for big data processing on single-node machines.
keyFeatures: Column-oriented data storage with native C++ implementation for all data types including strings
Expressive DT[i, j, by(...)] query syntax inspired by R's data.table
Multi-threaded data processing utilizing all CPU cores
Fast CSV/text/Excel reading with automatic format detection via fread()
Memory-mapped datasets for out-of-memory big data processing
Full support for null values across all data types
Efficient algorithms for sorting, grouping, and joining
Interoperability with pandas, numpy, pyarrow, and pure Python
Built-in FTRL and Linear Model machine learning algorithms
Binary .jay file format for fast disk storage and retrieval
publicApiSurface: dt.iread() — iterator for reading multiple files
dt.f / dt.g — column reference symbols for expressions
dt.union() / dt.intersect() / dt.setdiff() / dt.symdiff() — set operations
dt.cumsum() / dt.cummax() / dt.cummin() / dt.cumprod() — cumulative functions
dt.rowsum() / dt.rowmax() / dt.rowmin() / dt.rowmean() — row-wise aggregations
dt.mean() / dt.sum() / dt.min() / dt.max() / dt.sd() / dt.count() — aggregation functions
dt.stype / dt.ltype / dt.Type — data type system
